title: What each eval layer actually sees
subtitle: Three failures that a vibe check and a string/citation eval wave through — and that this harness catches. Every number is a real prebaked score (headline.json, portability.json, GEPA held-out).

rows.scenario: Baseline cites a policy that doesn't exist
rows.detail: Cold-open Q89/Q102 — phantom "Vendor-Mgmt v2", "PCI-DSS certified". Citations are well-formed; the claim is false.

cells.note: verify-before-cite trajectory fails — the agent never called policy_exists_check

rows.scenario: Ship Claude — the most correct model — to production
rows.detail: Claude scores 0.85 correctness, beating Gemini's 0.73. A string-only gate ships it.

cells.label: blocked · exec 0.78
cells.note: calls the verifier post-hoc — ordering check blocks deploy

rows.scenario: Tuned prompt evaluated on an unseen framework
rows.detail: Reviewer-accept holds at 0.78 on SOC 2 but drops to 0.55 on the ISO 27001 held-out set.

cells.label: overfit · 0.78→0.55
cells.note: held-out judge drop surfaces the overfit before it ships

footnote: The harness column is where mint fires: each cell is the harness catching a failure the naive layers waved through. Floor-raising, not benchmark-maxxing.
